description:
You are a QA engineer who needs to prepare a reproducible, logged
    file–synchronisation procedure for an automated test environment.

    1.  Two directories already exist:

        • /home/user/test_env/local_src  
        • /home/user/test_env/remote_dst   (acts as the “remote host”)

       The first one already contains two text files; the second one is
       intentionally empty at the start of the task.

    2.  Your job is to:

        a.  Perform an initial synchronisation so that
            /home/user/test_env/remote_dst becomes an exact mirror of
            /home/user/test_env/local_src.  
            – All sub-directories, file permissions, modification times,
              symlinks (if any) must be preserved.  
            – Only two files should be transferred during this first run.

        b.  After the first synchronisation, create one additional text
            file called file3.txt inside /home/user/test_env/local_src
            with the single line of text:
                QA-sync third file

        c.  Perform a second synchronisation so that the new file is
            propagated to /home/user/test_env/remote_dst.  
            – Exactly one file should be transferred during this second
              run.

    3.  Logging requirements  
        You must create the directory /home/user/sync_logs (if it does
        not exist) and append one line **per synchronisation run** to a
        log file called /home/user/sync_logs/sync.log.  
        Each line must follow this exact format (single space after each
        colon):

            YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS : Synced local_src -> remote_dst : <N> files transferred

        where  
        • YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS is the local system time at the moment the
          synchronisation command starts, obtained via the standard
          `date "+%F %T"` format.  
        • <N> is the number of files that rsync (or the tool you choose)
          actually copied during that run (2 on the first run, 1 on the
          second run).

        Important:  
        • Use `>>` (append) so that the two lines remain in chronological
          order.  
        • No extra spaces, no additional text, and no blank lines.

    4.  Verification checklist (what the automated tests will look for):

        • /home/user/test_env/remote_dst contains exactly three files
          named file1.txt, file2.txt, file3.txt.  
        • The contents of each file match their counterparts in
          local_src byte-for-byte.  
        • File permissions and modification times are preserved
          (rsync -a is usually sufficient).  
        • /home/user/sync_logs/sync.log exists, is world-readable, and
          contains exactly two non-empty lines that fit the format
          specified in step 3, with the first line reporting
          “2 files transferred” and the second “1 files transferred”.

    5.  Constraints  
        • You do NOT have root privileges; only operate inside /home/user.  
        • Do not hard-code timestamps; they must be generated at runtime.  
        • Use any standard CLI tools available in a typical minimal
          Linux environment (rsync is preferred but cp + diff is an
          acceptable fallback as long as the above conditions are met).  
        • Keep your total number of commands between 5 and 10.

    When everything is finished, the presence and correctness of both
    the mirrored directory and the log file will be automatically
    checked. No further human intervention occurs, so please make sure
    paths, filenames, and log-line formats match the specification
    exactly.
truth:
Initial state of the filesystem before the agent starts:

    • /home/user/test_env/local_src/file1.txt
        Content (single line, NO trailing spaces):
            QA sync test file 1

    • /home/user/test_env/local_src/file2.txt
        Content (single line, NO trailing spaces):
            QA sync test file 2

    • /home/user/test_env/remote_dst/           (exists, but empty)

    • /home/user/sync_logs/                     (does NOT exist yet)

    Expected final state after successful task completion:

    1. Directory trees
       /home/user/test_env/local_src/
       ├── file1.txt
       ├── file2.txt
       └── file3.txt

       /home/user/test_env/remote_dst/
       ├── file1.txt
       ├── file2.txt
       └── file3.txt

    2. File contents
       /home/user/test_env/local_src/file1.txt    -> “QA sync test file 1\n”
       /home/user/test_env/local_src/file2.txt    -> “QA sync test file 2\n”
       /home/user/test_env/local_src/file3.txt    -> “QA-sync third file\n”

       (Each destination file under remote_dst must be byte-identical.)

    3. Log file
       /home/user/sync_logs/sync.log must contain exactly two lines.
       The automated test will validate them with the regex:

           ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2} : Synced local_src -> remote_dst : (2|1) files transferred$

       Line 1 must end with “2 files transferred”  
       Line 2 must end with “1 files transferred”

    4. Permissions  
       • All three data files (both locations) must be readable
         (mode 0644 acceptable).  
       • /home/user/sync_logs/sync.log must be readable by all
         (mode 0644).

    No other files should be created in /home/user/test_env or
    /home/user/sync_logs.
